channel.title: Comments for page "Pseudohistorical Mischief"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Pseudohistorical Mischief"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No lasting civilization in Antarctica, you say?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rome was started by silly pranksters?

+1

Of course, I'm not sure I'd agree with any recent lasting civilization starting from the Antarctic, but this is meant to be humorous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read it a couple of times to make sure I wasn't missing something, and maybe I still am, but there seems to be a pretty big basic error. Correct me if I'm wrong, but…

These frat boys go to our reality the night before the guy's speech (or whenever, if it's time travel too I guess It's irrelevant) and basically insert the entire Roman culture/empire, profoundly altering our proceeding timeline. This then makes the head guys speech ridiculously inaccurate, which the dean, and entire audience, instantly realize. So my question is; why doesn't this guy, whose job is solely to study other realities, have this new timeline retconned into his head like apparently everyone else?

Again, if I'm missing something obvious, I apologize.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This took about three full readthroughs before I properly grasped it, and I liked it more with each read. That's a very good sign for me, but it might put off a less patient reader looking for quick thrills. Still a definite plus I from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bring up a very good point but consider this… perhaps the probation was not referred to because it was even more secret than double secret.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah, I learned a thing today.
English uses the Roman alphabet, with a few tweaks. So it does make perfect sense for the frat to be "greek".

Still wonder about the toga parties, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't believe the Romans used Greek letters as their alphabet, FWIW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would an AU frat use greek letters? That's their regular use alphabet, after all. You don't see many chapters of "Aye Bee Cee" running around on our side.
And I wonder what they do in place of toga parties, t-shirt keggers?

Anywho, an entertaining tidbit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cannot, in good conscience, upvote this story without the phrase "Double Secret Probation" in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the last line was a bit too silly for the rest of it, but regardless I enjoyed this. This is a great little crossover, plus I just straight up love good University material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just feel like Animal House hasn't aged well. It's one of those movies that's funny the first time you see it, but On repetition it just becomes kind of blah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
easier on the Animal House references

I don't want to live in whatever world you come from where this is a common practice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although the idea that humanity owns most of its troubles and strife to the Roman empire is irking in its utter inaccuracy.

It was more like "this alternate empire would have actually succeeded in unifying humanity, but oops, events hundreds of years prior made everything impossible", but I'll admit I didn't spend a whole lot of time mapping out the alternate histories in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, the Foundation universe was supposed to be a perfect utopia until a bunch of frat boys in scavenged costumes started the Roman Empire?

I think I like it. Although you could have gone easier on the Animal House references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, this sort of stuff I can live with. It's an alternate universe thing, and it's intentionally silly to boot. It's when people write incredibly stupid and inaccurate stuff about Rome which is supposed to be taken seriously that I get angry.

Although the idea that humanity owns most of its troubles and strife to the Roman empire is irking in its utter inaccuracy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "No vote" is a decent outcome actually, since I thought "man, Dmatix is going to jump all over me" every time I put in a reference even remotely related to the Roman Empire while writing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written, but I guess I'm too much of a Roman history nerd to like the idea of that empire being created by the University, rather than being an independently created alternate universe version of it. So, no vote it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Eskobar for allowing me to share this holiday-inspired foray into interdimensional silliness with the world.
